summary:
The major mechanism of action of nitrates in acute angina is decreasedpreload, primarily due to venodilation, which reduces cardiac work and myocardial oxygen demand. This remains the dominant pathway supporting rapid symptom relief compared to other effects like afterload reduction or coronary vasodilation.  

\boxed{B}
